question: What does hoti elthen he hora tes kriseos autou ("for the hour of his judgment is come") mean? What is this judgment -- the cross, the second coming, the investigative/pre-advent judgment, or something else? Why does the aorist elthen present it as an accomplished fact? How does krisis (G2920) differ from katakrima (G2631)? Trace krisis through the LXX to Daniel 7's dina. How does Daniel 7:22 establish this judgment is FOR believers? How can judgment be "good news"? Does the pre-advent judgment undermine assurance? Address using Daniel 12:1, 1 John 4:17, Romans 8:1,33-34, John 5:24. How does Romans 2:16 prove judgment IS gospel content?

topics.name: JUDGMENT
topics.entry_text: ACCORDING TO OPPORTUNITY AND WORKS GEN 4:7; JOB 34:11; PSA 62:12; MAT 25:14-30; ROM 2:5-12; 1CO 3:8,12-15; JAS 2:12,13; REV 2:23; 20:12,13

topics.name: ASSURANCE
topics.entry_text: SAINTS PRIVILEGED TO HAVE -- Eternal life 1JN 5:13. Unalienable love ROM 8:38,39. Peace ROM 5:1. Continuance PHP 1:6. Crown 2TI 4:7,8

topics.name: SATAN
topics.entry_text: The accuser REV 12:10. JOB 1:6-12; ZEC 3:1,2; REV 12:9-12

topics.name: GOSPEL
topics.entry_text: Gospel includes judgment: ROM 2:16; REV 14:6-7; LUK 3:18; ACT 17:30-31

verses.heading: The crux text: the hour of his judgment is come
verses.kjv_text: And I saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven, having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth, and to every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people, Saying with a loud voice, Fear God, and give glory to him; for the hour of his judgment is come: and worship him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the fountains of waters.
verses.context: First angel with everlasting gospel. Three imperatives + hoti clause declaring judgment arrival.
verses.notes: krisis G2920 genitive singular. elthen V-2AAI-3S prophetic aorist. hoti G3754 causal. Three aorist imperatives.

verses.heading: No katakrima -- condemnation negated, not judgment
verses.kjv_text: [There is] therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.
verses.context: Romans 8 after ch.7 struggle. katakrima G2631 NOT krisis G2920.

greek_parsing.gloss: Χριστῷ               Χριστός         G5547    [N-DSM] Dat Sg M

verses.notes: katakrima G2631 Nom Sg N. ouden categorical negation. Only 3 NT uses all in Romans.

verses.heading: Katakrima #1: from one sin to condemnation
verses.kjv_text: And not as [it was] by one that sinned, [so is] the gift: for the judgment [was] by one to condemnation, but the free gift [is] of many offences unto justification.
verses.context: Adam-Christ typology. krima from one to katakrima vs charisma to dikaioma.

verses.notes: krima G2917 -> katakrima G2631 vs charisma -> dikaioma. Text distinguishes process from adverse outcome.

verses.heading: Katakrima #2: one trespass, condemnation for all
verses.kjv_text: Therefore as by the offence of one [judgment came] upon all men to condemnation; even so by the righteousness of one [the free gift came] upon all men unto justification of life.
verses.context: Parallel: one paraptoma -> katakrima vs one dikaioma -> dikaiosis zoes.

verses.heading: All judgment (krisin) committed to the Son
verses.kjv_text: For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
verses.context: Father-Son discourse. ALL krisin given to Son. dedoken perfect tense.

greek_parsing.gloss: τὴν                  ὁ               G3588    [T-ASF] Acc Sg F

verses.notes: krisin G2920 Acc Sg F. dedoken V-RAI-3S perfect. pasan=ALL judgment permanently delegated.

verses.heading: Does not come into krisin -- condemnatory outcome
verses.kjv_text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
verses.context: After v.22. Hearing+believing=no krisis+passed from death to life.

verses.heading: Authority as Son of Man -- Dan 7:13 allusion
verses.kjv_text: And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man. Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice, And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
verses.context: Authority grounded in Son of Man identity. Dan 7:13-14 allusion. Resurrection judgment.
verses.notes: v.27 krisin anarthrous qualitative. Son of Man links Dan 7:13. v.29 resurrection of krisis vs life.

verses.heading: Boldness (parrhesia) IN the day of krisis
verses.kjv_text: Herein is our love made perfect, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment: because as he is, so are we in this world.
verses.context: Love perfected produces parrhesia in the day of krisis. Same word as Rev 14:7.

greek_parsing.gloss: τούτῳ                οὗτος           G3778    [D-DSN] Dat Sg N
παρρησίαν            παρρησία        G3954    [N-ASF] Acc Sg F

verses.notes: parrhesian G3954 Acc Sg F. en te hemera tes kriseos locative dative. Confidence WITHIN judgment.

verses.heading: Perfect love casts out fear -- kolasis fear
verses.kjv_text: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear: because fear hath torment. He that feareth is not made perfect in love.
verses.context: Fear cast out is kolasis-fear (punishment dread) not reverential awe.

verses.notes: phobos G5401. kolasin G2851 punishment. Only 2 NT uses. NOT phobeo of Rev 14:7.

verses.heading: OT source: heavenly court seated, books opened
verses.kjv_text: I beheld till the thrones were cast down, and the Ancient of days did sit, whose garment [was] white as snow, and the hair of his head like the pure wool: his throne [was like] the fiery flame, [and] his wheels [as] burning fire. A fiery stream issued and came forth from before him: thousand thousands ministered unto him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him: the judgment was set, and the books were opened.
verses.context: Night vision. Thrones, Ancient of Days, fiery stream, multitude. Court opens.
verses.notes: Aramaic: dina yetib A1780+A3488=court sat. siphrin pethichu=books opened. LXX: kriterion ekathisen G2922.

verses.heading: Son of Man to Ancient of Days -- dominion AFTER judgment
verses.kjv_text: I saw in the night visions, and, behold, [one] like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven, and came to the Ancient of days, and they brought him near before him. And there was given him dominion, and glory, and a kingdom, that all people, nations, and languages, should serve him: his dominion [is] an everlasting dominion, which shall not pass away, and his kingdom [that] which shall not be destroyed.
verses.context: After court scene. Son of Man TO Ancient of Days. Kingdom transfer after judgment.
verses.notes: Direction TO God. OG LXX uses latreuo. Kingdom follows judgment=pre-advent concept.

verses.heading: Judgment given TO/FOR the saints -- vindication
verses.kjv_text: Until the Ancient of days came, and judgment was given to the saints of the most High; and the time came that the saints possessed the kingdom.
verses.context: Horn wars saints UNTIL Ancient of Days comes, judgment given TO saints, saints possess kingdom.
verses.notes: Aramaic: dina yehib leqaddishe elyonin. le=benefactive. LXX: to krima edoken G2917. Three-phase: v.10 investigation, v.22 verdict, vv.26-27 execution.

verses.heading: Third judgment: dominion transferred to saints
verses.kjv_text: But the judgment shall sit, and they shall take away his dominion, to consume and to destroy [it] unto the end. And the kingdom and dominion, and the greatness of the kingdom under the whole heaven, shall be given to the people of the saints of the most High, whose kingdom [is] an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey him.
verses.context: Court sits, horn dominion removed, kingdom given to saints permanently.
verses.notes: dina yittib A1780+A3488 impf=court shall sit. LXX: to kriterion kathisei G2922.

verses.heading: Judgment IS gospel -- kata to euangelion mou
verses.kjv_text: In the day when God shall judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ according to my gospel.
verses.context: God judges secrets through Christ according to my gospel.

greek_parsing.gloss: ᾗ                    ὅς              G3739    [R-DSF] Dat Sg F
τὸ                   ὁ               G3588    [T-ASN] Acc Sg N
Χριστοῦ              Χριστός         G5547    [N-GSM] Gen Sg M

verses.notes: krinei G2919 V-PAI-3S present. kata G2596+euangelion G2098. Barnes/Meyer confirm kata modifies krinei.

verses.heading: Repentance commanded BECAUSE judgment appointed
verses.kjv_text: And the times of this ignorance God winked at; but now commandeth all men every where to repent: Because he hath appointed a day, in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by [that] man whom he hath ordained; [whereof] he hath given assurance unto all [men], in that he hath raised him from the dead.
verses.context: Mars Hill. Creator+command+judgment parallels Rev 14:7.
verses.notes: estesen hemeran=appointed day. mellei krinein=about to judge. Bridges anticipation(mellei)/announcement(elthen).

verses.heading: Judgment warnings AS gospel -- euengelizeto
verses.kjv_text: And many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people.
verses.context: Luke uses euengelizeto G2097 for judgment-inclusive preaching.
verses.notes: euengelizeto V-INI-3S imperfect. Judgment warnings=gospel proclamation.

verses.heading: Courtroom vindication: who charges? who condemns?
verses.kjv_text: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God's elect? [It is] God that justifieth. Who [is] he that condemneth? [It is] Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
verses.context: Triumphant courtroom rhetoric. Forensic: egkalesei, katakrinon, entygchanei.

verses.notes: egkalesei G1458 V-FAI-3S. katakrinon G2632. entygchanei G1793 V-PAI-3S present=ongoing intercession NOW.

verses.heading: Everyone FOUND WRITTEN delivered -- judgment as deliverance
verses.kjv_text: And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people: and there shall be a time of trouble, such as never was since there was a nation [even] to that same time: and at that time thy people shall be delivered, every one that shall be found written in the book.
verses.context: Michael stands for people. Deliverance for those found written in the book.
verses.notes: Names already IN book. Judgment FINDS and confirms. Verification not determination.

verses.heading: Fire tests works, person saved -- evaluative with safety net
verses.kjv_text: Every man's work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the fire shall try every man's work of what sort it is. If any man's work abide which he hath built thereupon, he shall receive a reward. If any man's work shall be burned, he shall suffer loss: but he himself shall be saved; yet so as by fire.
verses.context: Building metaphor. Fire tests erga. Even total work failure: person saved.
verses.notes: Fire tests erga G2041=same as Rev 20:12. Person saved sothesetai divine passive. Evaluative not condemnatory.

verses.heading: God investigates Sodom BEFORE judging
verses.kjv_text: And the LORD said, Because the cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grievous; I will go down now, and see whether they have done altogether according to the cry of it, which is come unto me; and if not, I will know.
verses.context: God uses investigation language despite omniscience. Transparent justice pattern.
verses.notes: Investigation for transparent justice. Pattern: cry->investigation->execution.

verses.heading: Wedding garment inspection -- pre-event examination
verses.kjv_text: And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment: And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless. Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast [him] into outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth. For many are called, but few [are] chosen.
verses.context: King inspects guests BEFORE feast. Three phases: invitation, inspection, removal.
verses.notes: eiselthon to theasasthai=came in to examine. Three-phase mirrors Dan 7.

verses.heading: God brings every work into judgment
verses.kjv_text: For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether [it be] good, or whether [it be] evil.
verses.context: After fear God (v.13). God brings EVERY work into mishpat.
verses.notes: mishpat H4941. Parallels Rev 14:7: fear God+judgment. Every secret=Rom 2:16 ta krupta.

verses.heading: Prophetic aorist parallel -- epesen epesen
verses.kjv_text: And there followed another angel, saying, Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city, because she made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication.
verses.context: Second angel. Same prophetic aorist. Doubled echoing Isa 21:9.

greek_parsing.gloss: ἄλλος                ἄλλος           G243     [A-NSM] Nom Sg M
Pres Act Ptcp Nom Sg M
τοῦ                  ὁ               G3588    [T-GSM] Gen Sg M

verses.notes: Epesen G4098 V-2AAI-3S doubled. Same grammar as elthen.

verses.heading: Courtroom vindication -- Joshua accused, defended, restored
verses.kjv_text: And he shewed me Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of the LORD, and Satan standing at his right hand to resist him. And the LORD said unto Satan, The LORD rebuke thee, O Satan; even the LORD that hath chosen Jerusalem rebuke thee: [is] not this a brand plucked out of the fire? Now Joshua was clothed with filthy garments, and stood before the angel. And he answered and spake unto those that stood before him, saying, Take away the filthy garments from him. And unto him he said, Behold, I have caused thine iniquity to pass from thee, and I will clothe thee with change of raiment. And I said, Let them set a fair mitre upon his head. So they set a fair mitre upon his head, and clothed him with garments. And the angel of the LORD stood by.
verses.context: Heavenly court. Joshua filthy garments, Satan accuses, God rebukes, garments replaced.
verses.notes: ha-satan H7854 formal accuser. Pattern: guilt->accusation->advocacy->cleansing->new status.

verses.heading: Great white throne -- books + book of life
verses.kjv_text: And I saw a great white throne, and him that sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away; and there was found no place for them. And I saw the dead, small and great, stand before God; and the books were opened: and another book was opened, which is [the book] of life: and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works. And the sea gave up the dead which were in it; and death and hell delivered up the dead which were in them: and they were judged every man according to their works. And death and hell were cast into the lake of fire. This is the second death. And whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire.
verses.context: Post-millennial. biblia (deeds) and biblos tes zoes. Judged kata ta erga.
verses.notes: biblia G975 books of deeds. kata ta erga auton G2041. Two standards: works AND book of life.

verses.heading: Book of remembrance preserves the faithful
verses.kjv_text: Then they that feared the LORD spake often one to another: and the LORD hearkened, and heard [it], and a book of remembrance was written before him for them that feared the LORD, and that thought upon his name. And they shall be mine, saith the LORD of hosts, in that day when I make up my jewels; and I will spare them, as a man spareth his own son that serveth him.
verses.context: Book of remembrance written FOR those who feared the LORD. segullah=special treasure.
verses.notes: sepher zikkaron H5612+H2146 benefactive. Records faithful service not sins.

verses.heading: God calls witnesses to judge His people
verses.kjv_text: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him. He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people. Gather my saints together unto me; those that have made a covenant with me by sacrifice. And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God [is] judge himself. Selah.
verses.context: Theophany of judgment. God gathers covenant saints. Heavens declare righteousness.
verses.notes: Judgment OF covenant community. Precedent for 1 Pet 4:17.

verses.heading: He comes to judge with righteousness -- positive event
verses.kjv_text: Before the LORD: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.
verses.context: Creation rejoices BECAUSE He comes to judge.
verses.notes: shaphat H8199. Judgment=reason for rejoicing when judge righteous.

verses.heading: LORD throne prepared for judgment -- righteous order
verses.kjv_text: But the LORD shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment. And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
verses.context: LORD endures forever, throne prepared for mishpat. Judges in righteousness.
verses.notes: mishpat H4941. meysharim=uprightness. Permanent righteous order.

verses.heading: Judgment begins at house of God
verses.kjv_text: For the time [is come] that judgment must begin at the house of God: and if [it] first [begin] at us, what shall the end [be] of them that obey not the gospel of God?
verses.context: krima G2917 begins at God house. Believers first. Pre-advent concept.
verses.notes: arx-=temporal priority. Believers evaluated FIRST.

verses.heading: Judgment of dead + ark revealed -- DOA activation
verses.kjv_text: And the nations were angry, and thy wrath is come, and the time of the dead, that they should be judged, and that thou shouldest give reward unto thy servants the prophets, and to the saints, and them that fear thy name, small and great; and shouldest destroy them which destroy the earth. And the temple of God was opened in heaven, and there was seen in his temple the ark of his testament: and there were lightnings, and voices, and thunderings, and an earthquake, and great hail.
verses.context: Seventh trumpet. Dead judged, servants rewarded. Temple opened, ark seen.
verses.notes: kibotos tes diathekes=ark of covenant DOA furniture. Three angels follow.

verses.heading: Bema of Christ -- ALL must appear
verses.kjv_text: For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things [done] in [his] body, according to that he hath done, whether [it be] good or bad.
verses.context: ALL appear before bema for evaluation of deeds.
verses.notes: bema G968. pantas G3956 ALL including believers.

verses.heading: Sanctuary vindicated -- timing question
verses.kjv_text: And he said unto me, Unto two thousand and three hundred days; then shall the sanctuary be cleansed.
verses.context: 2300 days. Sanctuary nitsdaq. Concept from Dan 7 independent of timing.
verses.notes: nitsdaq niphal of tsadaq=vindicated. Pre-advent CONCEPT from Dan 7 sequence.

verses.heading: Law of liberty -- mercy triumphs
verses.kjv_text: So speak ye, and so do, as they that shall be judged by the law of liberty. For he shall have judgment without mercy, that hath shewed no mercy; and mercy rejoiceth against judgment.
verses.context: Judged by law of liberty. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
verses.notes: nomos eleutheiras. katakauchastai G2620 mercy triumphs.

verses.heading: Accuser cast down
verses.kjv_text: And I heard a loud voice saying in heaven, Now is come salvation, and strength, and the kingdom of our God, and the power of his Christ: for the accuser of our brethren is cast down, which accused them before our God day and night.
verses.context: Accuser (kategor) of brethren cast down. Overcome by blood of Lamb.
verses.notes: kategor G2725 prosecutor disbarred. ebletke aorist passive.

verses.heading: God questions before judgment -- investigation pattern
verses.kjv_text: And they heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God amongst the trees of the garden. And the LORD God called unto Adam, and said unto him, Where [art] thou? And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I [was] naked; and I hid myself. And he said, Who told thee that thou [wast] naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat? And the man said, The woman whom thou gavest [to be] with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat. And the LORD God said unto the woman, What [is] this [that] thou hast done? And the woman said, The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.
verses.context: After fall. God calls, asks, investigates BEFORE pronouncing judgment.
verses.notes: Questions for transparent process. Pattern: presence->inquiry->response->verdict.

verses.heading: Just and true are thy ways -- vindication progression
verses.kjv_text: And they sing the song of Moses the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and marvellous [are] thy works, Lord God Almighty; just and true [are] thy ways, thou King of saints. Who shall not fear thee, O Lord, and glorify thy name? for [thou] only [art] holy: for all nations shall come and worship before thee; for thy judgments are made manifest.
verses.context: Song of Moses/Lamb. Character affirmation. Fear+glorify echo 14:7.
verses.notes: 14:7 announcement->15:3-4 character->16:7 acts->19:1-2 completion.

verses.heading: True and righteous are thy judgments
verses.kjv_text: And I heard another out of the altar say, Even so, Lord God Almighty, true and righteous [are] thy judgments.
verses.context: During plagues. Altar affirms specific judgment acts.
verses.notes: kriseis G2920 plural. Altar=martyrs (6:9-11) vindicated.

verses.heading: Vindication complete -- he hath judged
verses.kjv_text: And after these things I heard a great voice of much people in heaven, saying, Alleluia; Salvation, and glory, and honour, and power, unto the Lord our God: For true and righteous [are] his judgments: for he hath judged the great whore, which did corrupt the earth with her fornication, and hath avenged the blood of his servants at her hand.
verses.context: After Babylon fall. Final vindication formula.
verses.notes: ekrinen G2919 aorist completed. Full progression announcement to completion.

verses.heading: We shall all stand before the bema of God -- universal accountability
verses.kjv_text: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ. For it is written, [As] I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God. So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.
verses.context: Paul addresses mutual judgment among believers. Every person will stand before God's judgment seat and give personal account.
verses.notes: bema tou Christou (judgment seat of Christ). Universal: every knee, every tongue, every one of us. Confirms believers face evaluative accounting.

verses.heading: Appointed unto men once to die, then judgment -- universal appointment
verses.kjv_text: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:
verses.context: Within Hebrews argument about Christ's once-for-all sacrifice. Judgment follows death as a universal human appointment.
verses.notes: apokeitai (G606) 'it is appointed/laid up.' krisis (G2920) — same word as Rev 14:7. Death and judgment are parallel appointments.

verses.heading: Fearful expectation of judgment for willful sin -- the serious side
verses.kjv_text: For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins, But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation, which shall devour the adversaries. He that despised Moses' law died without mercy under two or three witnesses: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace? For we know him that hath said, Vengeance [belongeth] unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.
verses.context: Hebrews warning about willful apostasy after receiving truth. Judgment is fearful for those who trample the Son of God.
verses.notes: phobera ekdoche kriseos — fearful expectation of judgment. The serious side: judgment threatens the apostate. But note v.30 'the Lord shall judge his people' — echoes Deut 32:36, judgment OF the covenant community.

verses.heading: Great day of wrath -- those who hide from judgment contrast with 1 John 4:17 boldness
verses.kjv_text: And the kings of the earth, and the great men, and the rich men, and the chief captains, and the mighty men, and every bondman, and every free man, hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains; And said to the mountains and rocks, Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him that sitteth on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb: For the great day of his wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?
verses.context: Sixth seal. Universal terror before the Lamb's wrath. The wicked hide — contrasts sharply with 1 John 4:17's boldness in judgment.
verses.notes: elthen he hemera he megale tes orges — 'the great day of his wrath IS COME' — same prophetic aorist construction as Rev 14:7 elthen he hora. The question 'who shall be able to stand?' is answered by those with parrhesia (1 John 4:17).

verses.heading: Every idle word -- by words justified or condemned; comprehensive judgment
verses.kjv_text: But I say unto you, That every idle word that men shall speak, they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment. For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
verses.context: Jesus warns the Pharisees about accountability for speech. Every idle word enters the judgment record.
verses.notes: en hemera kriseos — in the day of judgment (same krisis as Rev 14:7). pan rhema argon — every idle/careless word. Judgment is comprehensive — includes speech, not just deeds. dikaiothese / katadikasthese — justified or condemned — two possible outcomes.

verses.heading: Christ ever lives to make intercession -- ongoing high priestly ministry
verses.kjv_text: Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.
verses.context: Christ's permanent, unchangeable priesthood. He saves completely because He always lives to intercede.
verses.notes: entygchanein (G1793 infinitive) — to make intercession. panteles (G3838) — to the uttermost/completely. pantote zon (always living) — present participle, continuous. Christ's intercession is ONGOING, not past — active during any judgment period.

verses.heading: Will not blot out name from book of life -- preservation promise during judgment
verses.kjv_text: He that overcometh, the same shall be clothed in white raiment; and I will not blot out his name out of the book of life, but I will confess his name before my Father, and before his angels.
verses.context: Promise to the overcomer in Sardis. Names preserved in the book of life + confessed before the Father.
verses.notes: ou me exaleipso — emphatic double negative: 'I will absolutely NOT blot out.' biblos tes zoes — book of life. homologeso — I will confess/acknowledge. Preservation promise directly linked to the judgment book imagery.

verses.heading: Book of life of the Lamb slain from foundation -- names written before judgment
verses.kjv_text: And all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him, whose names are not written in the book of life of the Lamb slain from the foundation of the world.
verses.context: The beast's universal worship. Those NOT written in the book of life worship the beast. Names inscribed from the foundation of the world.
verses.notes: bibliō tēs zōēs tou arniou — book of life of the Lamb. apo katabolēs kosmou — from the foundation of the world. Names inscribed BEFORE judgment, not determined by it.

verses.heading: Names are in the book of life -- present-tense assurance of inscription
verses.kjv_text: And I intreat thee also, true yokefellow, help those women which laboured with me in the gospel, with Clement also, and [with] other my fellowlabourers, whose names [are] in the book of life.
verses.context: Paul mentions fellow workers whose names ARE (present tense) in the book of life. Present assurance of inscription.
verses.notes: hōn ta onomata en bibliō zōēs — whose names [are] in the book of life. Present-tense statement of current inscription. Assurance coexists with judgment awareness.

verses.heading: Let them be blotted from the book of the living -- OT book-of-life background
verses.kjv_text: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.
verses.context: David's imprecatory prayer. The 'book of the living' can have names blotted from it — OT background for Rev 3:5's preservation promise.
verses.notes: sepher chayyim (H5612 + H2416) — book of the living. yimmachuw — let them be blotted out. Names CAN be removed — making the Rev 3:5 promise meaningful. The judgment confirms or removes names.

verses.heading: In thy book all my members were written -- God's comprehensive knowledge recorded
verses.kjv_text: Thine eyes did see my substance, yet being unperfect; and in thy book all [my members] were written, [which] in continuance were fashioned, when [as yet there was] none of them.
verses.context: David on God's intimate, comprehensive knowledge. Everything written in God's book before it existed.
verses.notes: siphreka (H5612 with suffix) — in your book. God's omniscient record-keeping. The books of judgment reflect this comprehensive divine knowledge.

verses.heading: When thy judgments are in the earth, inhabitants learn righteousness
verses.kjv_text: With my soul have I desired thee in the night; yea, with my spirit within me will I seek thee early: for when thy judgments [are] in the earth, the inhabitants of the world will learn righteousness.
verses.context: Isaiah's song of trust. God's judgments in the earth produce a pedagogical/redemptive effect — the world learns righteousness.
verses.notes: mishpat (H4941) — judgments. tsedeq (H6664) — righteousness. Judgment as pedagogical: inhabitants LEARN righteousness through God's judgments. Redemptive, not merely punitive.

verses.heading: Seventy weeks -- atonement-to-righteousness progression linked to judgment timing
verses.kjv_text: Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city, to finish the transgression, and to make an end of sins, and to make reconciliation for iniquity, and to bring in everlasting righteousness, and to seal up the vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most Holy.
verses.context: Gabriel's seventy-weeks prophecy. Six purposes: finish transgression, end sins, reconcile iniquity, bring everlasting righteousness, seal vision, anoint Most Holy.
verses.notes: kaphar (H3722) — make reconciliation/atonement. tsedeq olamim — everlasting righteousness. The kaphar-to-tsedeq progression links DOA atonement to judgment timing per the dan-7-judgment-antitypical-doa study.

verses.heading: Day of Atonement -- on this day atonement made to cleanse; typological basis
verses.kjv_text: For on that day shall [the priest] make an atonement for you, to cleanse you, [that] ye may be clean from all your sins before the LORD.
verses.context: The Day of Atonement ritual. Atonement (kaphar) produces cleansing (taher) from all sins before the LORD.
verses.notes: kaphar (H3722) — atone/cover. taher (H2891) — cleanse/purify. The DOA typology: ritual cleansing maps to Dan 7 forensic judgment per Lev 16:30 kaphar -> Dan 8:14 nitsdaq progression.

verses.heading: Sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise -- arrabon guarantee through judgment
verses.kjv_text: In whom ye also [trusted], after that ye heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation: in whom also after that ye believed, ye were sealed with that holy Spirit of promise, Which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession, unto the praise of his glory.
verses.context: Paul on the believer's security. The Holy Spirit is a seal (sphragis) and earnest (arrabon) — a deposit guaranteeing the inheritance through any judgment.
verses.notes: esphragisthete (G4972 aorist passive) — you were sealed. arrabon (G728) — earnest/down payment/guarantee. The Spirit as guarantee extends THROUGH any judgment period — the seal is not removed by evaluation.

verses.heading: That you may KNOW you have eternal life -- oida settled certainty alongside judgment
verses.kjv_text: These things have I written unto you that believe on the name of the Son of God; that ye may know that ye have eternal life, and that ye may believe on the name of the Son of God.
verses.context: John's stated purpose for writing. Believers can KNOW (oida, settled certainty) they have eternal life.
verses.notes: eidete (G1492 perfect active subjunctive) — 'that you may know' with settled certainty. The same author who writes about boldness in judgment day (4:17) also writes about settled assurance of eternal life. These coexist.

verses.heading: Crown of righteousness from the righteous judge -- judgment as reward
verses.kjv_text: I have fought a good fight, I have finished [my] course, I have kept the faith: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.
verses.context: Paul's final testimony. The 'righteous judge' (dikaios krites) awards a crown — judgment as REWARD, not threat.
verses.notes: ho dikaios krites (G1342 + G2923) — the righteous judge. stephanos tes dikaiosynes — crown of righteousness. apodosei (G591 future) — will give/award. Judgment produces REWARD for the faithful. The judge's character (dikaios) guarantees fair outcome.

verses.heading: The Lord CAME (elthen) with ten thousands -- parallel prophetic aorist
verses.kjv_text: And Enoch also, the seventh from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord cometh with ten thousands of his saints, To execute judgment upon all, and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed, and of all their hard [speeches] which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.
verses.context: Jude quotes Enochic tradition. The Lord 'came' (elthen, same prophetic aorist as Rev 14:7) with myriads to execute judgment.
verses.notes: elthen kyrios (G2064 V-2AAI-3S) — same prophetic aorist form as Rev 14:7 elthen. poiesai krisin (G2920) — to execute judgment. Enochic tradition uses the same grammatical device: future judgment narrated as past.

verses.heading: Kingdoms became our Lord's (egeneto) -- prophetic aorist parallel
verses.kjv_text: And the seventh angel sounded; and there were great voices in heaven, saying, The kingdoms of this world are become [the kingdoms] of our Lord, and of his Christ; and he shall reign for ever and ever.
verses.context: Seventh trumpet. The kingdom transfer announced as accomplished fact — egeneto (became/has become), prophetic aorist.
verses.notes: egeneto (G1096 V-2ADI-3S) — aorist indicative 'became.' Same prophetic aorist pattern as elthen in Rev 14:7 and epesen in Rev 14:8. Kingdom transfer announced as fait accompli before the events of chs.12-20 unfold.

word_studies.summary: Judgment/evaluation PROCESS. 47 NT occ. From krino. Judicial process -- separating, trial, evaluation.
word_studies.relevance: THE word in Rev 14:7. Angel announces judgment PROCESS not condemnation.

word_studies.summary: Adverse verdict+execution. Only 3 NT occ (Rom 5:16,5:18,8:1). Uniformly condemnation.
word_studies.relevance: CONTRASTING word. Rom 8:1 no katakrima not no krisis. Exempt from condemnation not evaluation.

word_studies.summary: Judgment/decision RESULT. 27 NT occ. LXX for mishpat 175x. Dan 7:22 LXX term.
word_studies.relevance: LXX term in Dan 7:22 to krima edoken. Bridges PROCESS and OUTCOME.

word_studies.summary: Root verb to judge/separate/decide. 132 tokens across NT.
word_studies.relevance: ROOT VERB. Rom 2:16 God judges according to gospel.
LXX for COURT in Dan 7:10 kriterion ekathisen and 7:26.
Completes krin- family: judge, krisis, krima, kriterion.

word_studies.summary: To condemn. 19 NT verses. Always adverse.
word_studies.relevance: Rom 8:34 who condemns? Christ intercedes.

word_studies.summary: Act of condemning. Only 2 NT occ.
word_studies.relevance: Completes kata-krin- family.
INVESTIGATIVE verb. Investigation built into krin- word group.
Completes krin- family mapping.

word_studies.summary: Aramaic judgment/court. 5 OT occ. Dan 7 SOURCE WORD.
word_studies.relevance: SOURCE feeding entire krisis chain.
Hebrew cognate of Aramaic A1780.
Verbal root behind A1780 and H1779.

word_studies.summary: Most common OT judgment term. 448 tokens. LXX krima 175x + krisis 132x.
word_studies.relevance: Primary OT bridge to NT krisis. Eccl 12:14 parallels Rev 14:7.
OT verb bridge to NT krino.

word_studies.summary: Covenant lawsuit verb. 79 tokens. LXX krisis 29x.
word_studies.relevance: riyb->krisis LXX bridge. Rev 14:7 carries covenant lawsuit weight.
Noun form. LXX renders as krisis 29x.

word_studies.summary: Hour/appointed time. 108 occ. With article=THE appointed hour.
word_studies.relevance: Rev 14:7 he hora. Parallels Jesus' hora language.
elthen V-2AAI-3S prophetic aorist. Compare Jude 14.
1 John 4:17 parrhesia IN day of krisis. Answer to assurance question.
1 John 4:18 kolasis fear cast out, NOT reverential awe.

word_studies.summary: Fear -- alarm or reverence. 44 NT verses.
word_studies.relevance: 1 John 4:18 phobos (dread) vs Rev 14:7 phobeo (reverence).
Rev 14:7 Phobethete decisive reverential command.
1 John 2:1 Christ as defense attorney in judgment.
Rom 8:34 present tense ONGOING intercession during judgment.
Triple intercession Rom 8: Spirit v.26, Spirit v.27, Christ v.34.

word_studies.summary: Mediator. 6 NT occ. Christ new covenant mediator.
word_studies.relevance: Christ stands between God and humanity during judgment.
Embeds righteousness into judgment concept.
Root of dik- family.
Rev 14:6 euangelion aionion. Rom 2:16 kata to euangelion mou.
Luke 3:18 euengelizeto with judgment content.
Rev 20:12 biblia of deeds. Books contain erga broadly.
Dan 12:1 found written. Mal 3:16 sepher zikkaron.
Rev 14:7 give glory. Glory-judgment connection.
Rev 20:12-13 judged according to erga. Includes faithful service.

1 John 4:17 en te hemera tes kriseos. Rev 14:7 uses hora not hemera.
Connects DOA typology to judgment.
Rom 8:33 who shall charge? God justifies.
Rev 12:10 Satan as prosecutor cast down.
Zech 3:1 ha-satan in courtroom.
dina yetib=court took seat.
2 Tim 4:8 righteous judge.
Gods dikaiosyne guarantees fair krisis.
Rom 8:33 God who justifies. Present=ongoing.
Rom 5:18 katakrima vs dikaiosis. Opposite outcomes.
